payer_name: CVS Caremark Non-Medicare Formulary Exception / Prior Authorization Request Form
supportive_texts: Drug-specific rules (DS$FR#1, DS$FR#4, DS$FR#5) for Zepbound state that for these products the medication is used with lifestyle modifications including a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.
FR#37 mandates answering 'Yes' for this question.
Prolonged use of Zepbound with stability at 7.5 mg for >3 months after resuming in late Dec 2025 until the 5/12/2026 increase to 10 mg.
10 mg started on 5/12/2026 has not been 3 months yet, but the dose does not need to match exactly.
The patient has completed ≥3 months on the requested drug at a stable maintenance dose.
The prescription is for Zepbound (tirzepatide), not Wegovy (semaglutide).
The chart shows obesity (BMI 34.5) and hypertension.
The chart documents obesity (BMI 34.5; ICD-10 E66.9).
There is a request for weight loss medication (Zepbound).
contradictory_texts: No established cardiovascular disease (no MI, stroke, PAD, or revascularization) is present.
There is no diagnosis or documentation of obstructive sleep apnea.
There is no sleep study indicating moderate to severe OSA.
The prescription requests Zepbound (tirzepatide) for obesity (E66.9).
There is no documentation of Wegovy (semaglutide) being requested.

supportive_texts: The EMR lists ICD-10 D50.9 (iron deficiency anemia, unspecified).
Provider notes indicate iron deficiency due to chronic heavy menstrual bleeding.
No CKD or heart failure is indicated.
Provider notes document “She has been taking oral iron without improvement.”
Provider notes document “persistent symptoms despite oral iron.”
FR#4 mandates strictly selecting expedited review when given the choice between standard and expedited review.
DS$FR#3 specifically covers Injectafer.
Substitution with the preferred brand or generic on the formulary is always permitted.
Diagnosis listed: D50.9 – Iron deficiency anemia, unspecified.
Injectafer (ferric carboxymaltose) is FDA-approved for treatment of iron deficiency anemia.
contradictory_texts: No specific medication names other than "oral iron via prenatal vitamin" are listed.
No specific outcomes other than "inadequate response" are detailed.
Signed by Alexander Zuhoski, MD and Melanie Acierno, DNP.
There is no information provided about whether the member will be taking any additional medication therapy for this diagnosis.

supportive_texts: Per Fixed Response rule FR#4, when given the choice between standard and expedited review, strictly select expedited.
DS$FR#3 directly applies for Venofer.
The drug-specific fixed response states substitution with the preferred brand or generic on the formulary is allowed.
Drug-specific rules override general FR rules.
Venofer (iron sucrose) is FDA-approved for iron deficiency anemia in patients with chronic kidney disease.
contradictory_texts: No documented stability or sustained response yet.

payer_name: CVS Caremark Non-Medicare Formulary Exception / Prior Authorization Request Form
supportive_texts: The request is for Wegovy.
Per FR#37 (and DS$FR#4/DS$FR#5 for Wegovy), when asked whether the drug will be used with a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity, the fixed response is "Yes."
DOB 10/12/1977 documented.
As of 2026-06-08 the patient is 48 years old.
The patient has been on Wegovy at a stable maintenance dose of 2.4 mg weekly since at least 11/20/2025.
FR#35 applies: the rules state that when asked this specific question about plan coverage for other drugs, the required fixed response is "No".
Documentation shows only prior oxybutynin ER use.
There is no documented intolerance, contraindication, or inadequate response to oxybutynin.
There are no trials/failures with mirabegron (Myrbetriq), solifenacin, tolterodine, trospium, or other listed formulary options.
Only oxybutynin ER trial is documented.
contradictory_texts: The patient prefers to stay off oxybutynin ER, but this is not documented as intolerance or contraindication.
No failures or intolerances to other formulary alternatives are documented.
No contraindications to other formulary alternatives are recorded.

payer_name: Caremark Medicare Coverage Determination Request Form
supportive_texts: FR#4 states to strictly select expedited request when given the choice between standard and expedited review.
The prescription is tadalafil 5 mg daily with diagnosis N52.9 (male erectile dysfunction).
Clinical notes post-RALP document poor erections and “start Cialis.”
